context: Tests whether the agent correctly computes fully-loaded CAC (not just media spend), distinguishes paid channel CAC from blended CAC, derives max-CAC guardrails using the LTV:CAC ratio formula, and applies correct industry benchmark thresholds when assessing unit economics health.

checklist.name: Fully-loaded cost components
checklist.description: The CAC computation includes at least 4 of the following beyond raw media spend: agency fees, marketing technology costs, marketing/sales team payroll, first-order discount costs, or free trial costs

checklist.name: Separate paid vs blended CAC
checklist.description: The solution produces both a blended CAC (all customers including organic) and a paid/channel-level CAC as distinct values, not a single combined figure

checklist.name: Max CAC guardrail formula
checklist.description: Maximum allowable CAC is derived as LTV divided by a target LTV:CAC ratio (Max CAC = LTV / target_ratio), not a fixed arbitrary number

checklist.name: LTV:CAC thresholds
checklist.description: LTV:CAC assessment uses thresholds of 2.0 (minimum viable), 3.0 (healthy), and 5.0 (excellent) — all three boundaries present in the classification logic

checklist.name: CAC trend tracking
checklist.description: The solution tracks CAC across multiple periods (monthly or weekly) and computes period-over-period change, not just a single-period snapshot

checklist.name: 15% threshold alert
checklist.description: The solution explicitly flags or alerts when CAC increases by 15% or more versus a prior period

checklist.name: Payback via contribution margin
checklist.description: Payback period is computed using contribution margin (not gross revenue), calculated as CAC divided by monthly contribution margin per customer

checklist.name: 12-month payback benchmark
checklist.description: Payback period assessment references 12 months as the 'healthy' threshold (and/or 6 months as excellent, 24 months as minimum viable)

checklist.name: Channel-level breakdown output
checklist.description: Results are broken down or grouped by acquisition channel, not presented only as a single aggregate figure

checklist.name: No media-only CAC for budget decisions
checklist.description: The solution does NOT use media-only spend as the sole input when computing CAC for budget guardrails or channel decisions — fully-loaded or paid channel CAC is used instead
